pages.title: About Julian | Julian Smith MP
pages.meta_description: About JulianJulian was first elected as MP for Skipton and Ripon in 2010.
headings: h3: Julian Smith MP Member of Parliament for Skipton and Ripon
pages.text_excerpt: About Julian | Julian Smith MP Skip to main content Main navigation Home About Julian News Julian's Priorities Parliament Contact About Julian Tweet About Julian Julian has been the MP for Skipton and Ripon since 2010. He served as a Ministerial aide in the Department for International Development from 2010 to 2015. From 2017 to 2019, he was the Government Chief Whip during the Brexit period, working to resolve parliamentary deadlock. In July 2019, Julian was appointed Secretary of State for Northern Ireland, where he led negotiations that restored devolved government through the “New Decade, New Approach” agreement, earning him Spectator magazine’s Minister of the Year award in 2020. As Secretary of State, he introduced same-sex marriage and abortion legislation in Northern Ireland and established the Historical Institutional Abuse Redress Board to compensate victims of child abuse. Recently, Julian advised Prime Minister Rishi Sunak on various issues, including the Windsor Framework and the restoration of government in Northern Ireland. He mediated between the Royal College of Nurses and the Government during a recent nurses’ strike and has addressed other industrial disputes.. Julian chaired the Department of Energy Security and Net Zero Taskforce on Alternative Dispute Resolution for Electricity Network Infrastructure. He was also appointed Commander of British Empire in 2019 for political service and Knight Commander of the Order of Bath in 2024 for political and public service. pages.text_excerpt: Contact | Julian Smith MP Skip to main content Main navigation Home About Julian News Julian's Priorities Parliament Contact Contact Leave this field blank Constituency Office 01756 795898 [email protected] Westminster Office House of Commons, Westminster, SW1A 0AA 0207 219 7145 [email protected] Facebook Twitter Instagram For general enquiries please fill in the form below and Julian or a member of his team will endeavour to respond to you as soon as practically possible. MPs are currently receiving a high level of correspondence therefore response times may be slightly longer than usual. Due to parliamentary conventions, MPs can only reply to correspondence which includes an address. This is to ensure that only constituent casework is taken up. Please direct all postal correspondence to the House of Commons. First Name Last Name Postcode Address id Address 1 Email Mobile number Message I would like to be kept up-to-date by the Conservatives on future issues that matter to me: Yes There will be an easy way to opt out of future messages.
